template_vars.question:
What is the capital of Japan?
A) Seoul
B) Tokyo
C) Beijing
D) Bangkok

What is the chemical formula for water?
A) CO2
B) O2
C) H2O
D) NaCl

How many continents are there on Earth?
A) 5
B) 6
C) 7
D) 8

Which is the largest planet in the Solar System?
A) Earth
B) Saturn
C) Jupiter
D) Neptune

Who wrote the play "Romeo and Juliet"?
A) Charles Dickens
B) William Shakespeare
C) Mark Twain
D) Ernest Hemingway

Which gas makes up the largest fraction of Earth's atmosphere?
A) Oxygen
B) Carbon dioxide
C) Nitrogen
D) Hydrogen

How many sides does a hexagon have?
A) 5
B) 6
C) 7
D) 8

At what temperature does water freeze at sea level, in degrees Celsius?
A) -10
B) 0
C) 32
D) 100

The Great Barrier Reef lies off the coast of which country?
A) Brazil
B) Australia
C) Mexico
D) Egypt

Which of these travels fastest?
A) Sound in air
B) Light in a vacuum
C) A commercial jet
D) A high-speed train

Which element has atomic number 6?
A) Oxygen
B) Carbon
C) Nitrogen
D) Helium

In which year did World War II end?
A) 1918
B) 1939
C) 1945
D) 1950

Which is the largest ocean on Earth?
A) Atlantic
B) Indian
C) Arctic
D) Pacific

Who painted the Mona Lisa?
A) Michelangelo
B) Leonardo da Vinci
C) Raphael
D) Donatello

Which organelle is commonly called the powerhouse of the cell?
A) Nucleus
B) Ribosome
C) Mitochondrion
D) Golgi apparatus

What is the square root of 144?
A) 11
B) 12
C) 13
D) 14

Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
A) Venus
B) Mars
C) Jupiter
D) Mercury

What is the official currency of Japan?
A) Yuan
B) Won
C) Yen
D) Ringgit

How many bones are in the adult human body?
A) 196
B) 206
C) 216
D) 226

Which gas do plants primarily absorb from the air for photosynthesis?
A) Oxygen
B) Nitrogen
C) Carbon dioxide
D) Methane

What is the atomic number of gold?
A) 47
B) 79
C) 82
D) 29

In which year did Einstein publish the general theory of relativity?
A) 1905
B) 1915
C) 1921
D) 1925

In which year was the Peace of Westphalia signed?
A) 1618
B) 1648
C) 1715
D) 1789

What is the most abundant element in the universe by mass?
A) Helium
B) Oxygen
C) Hydrogen
D) Carbon

Which blood type is considered the universal donor for red blood cell transfusions?
A) AB positive
B) O negative
C) A positive
D) B negative

What is the SI unit of electrical resistance?
A) Volt
B) Ampere
C) Ohm
D) Watt

Which mathematician is best known for the incompleteness theorems?
A) David Hilbert
B) Kurt Godel
C) Alan Turing
D) Georg Cantor

What is the capital of Australia?
A) Sydney
B) Melbourne
C) Canberra
D) Perth

The chemical symbol "K" stands for which element?
A) Krypton
B) Potassium
C) Calcium
D) Phosphorus

What is the worst-case time complexity of binary search on a sorted array of n elements?
A) O(n)
B) O(log n)
C) O(n log n)
D) O(1)
